title: A Dravidian solution to the Indus script problem

claims.normalized_claim: Indus script encodes Proto-Dravidian language using the rebus principle.
claims.falsification_condition: If Dravidian phonotactics do not fit Indus sign bigrams better than Sanskrit.
claims.glossa_lab_evidence: Phase-41: 1.0566x SA advantage; Phase-43: +0.484 log-units on V3 corpus [VERIFIED]

claims.normalized_claim: The fish sign (meen in Dravidian) is the primary rebus sign in the Indus script.
claims.proposed_value: meen/min (fish; also star, lightning in Proto-Dravidian)
claims.falsification_condition: If fish signs do not show terminal-sign followers consistent with case suffixes.
